reports.title: The Islamabad Gamble
reports.subtitle: Vance flew to Pakistan to negotiate a peace with Iran that neither side can fully afford to want.

reports.title: The Tariff That Outlived Its Legal Theory
reports.subtitle: Trump's 10% global tariff is standing on a 1974 law the Supreme Court already gutted, and a panel of judges just told him so.

reports.title: Shutdown Theater
reports.subtitle: DHS ordered furloughed workers back to their desks during a shutdown, which is either a constitutional violation or proof that 'shutdown' no longer means what it used to.

reports.title: Two Parties, Two Theories of What AI Is
reports.subtitle: Both parties want to regulate AI, but they disagree on whether the problem is the technology itself or what people do with it, and that gap cannot be bridged.

reports.title: 32 Hours of Nothing
reports.subtitle: Putin announced a ceasefire for Orthodox Easter. Ukraine accepted. The war did not.

reports.title: Beijing's Proxy in Taipei
reports.subtitle: Xi met the KMT's leader in Beijing while 16 Chinese warplanes circled Taiwan. The timing was not coincidental.

reports.title: The Toll Booth at the End of the War
reports.subtitle: The ceasefire is two weeks old. The Strait of Hormuz is still closed. Iran is charging ships for the privilege of passing through.

reports.title: Supremely Intelligent Teenagers
reports.subtitle: Seventy-nine percent of enterprises are running AI agents. Eleven percent have security approval for what those agents can do. The gap is not a growing pain.

reports.title: The Law That Teenagers Broke in a Week
reports.subtitle: Australia's under-16 social media ban has been in force for four months. The regulator says platforms are failing. A 15-year-old named Noah is fighting it in court. Both things can be true.

reports.title: The Government That Pressed the Mute Button
reports.subtitle: The DOJ settled a lawsuit confirming the Biden State Department funded tools to suppress protected speech on social media. The settlement admits more than it resolves.
